prompt: Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition. Combine the two reference images: from the FIRST reference take the vial geometry, proportions, silhouette, and pure white seamless studio background with soft shadow (a clear borosilicate glass 5ml vial with stockier cylindrical body, pronounced shoulder, short narrow neck). From the SECOND reference take the EXACT cap color, label design, typography, and bottom accent bar — matching them pixel-faithfully. Cap color must be EXACTLY hex #101850 (navy blue) — match this hex code precisely, do not drift lighter or darker, do not shift hue. The vial is sealed with a gray rubber injection stopper crimped under a navy blue aluminum collar cap and matching flip-off button at exactly #101850. SIMPLIFIED LABEL DESIGN: matte-white pharmaceutical label wrapped around the lower two-thirds of the body. Upper roughly 75% of the label is a generous blank pristine white logo zone — absolutely no graphics, no text, no boxes, no hairlines, no rules, no dashed lines, no marker shapes, no logo placeholder symbols. Below that, the product name "BPC-157" centered in clean modern medium-bold geometric medical sans-serif typography in dark charcoal grey, natural letter proportions (NOT vertically stretched, NOT condensed). At the very bottom edge of the label, a slim solid accent bar at exactly hex #101850 — the only line on the label. NO hairlines anywhere. The composition shows the 5ml vial centered in the frame on pure white background. Sharp focus, high resolution, premium pharmaceutical product shot.
